entry.title: Re: Transport Iflows between different packages in the same SAP Cloud Integration tenant
entry.content:
Hi @former_member9237  ,

I tried with the given groovy script, but facing unable to resolve class error. Can you please help me on this issue.

 

Regards,

Namratha Raj

entry.title: Re: From Monday Blues to Data Delights: My Geeky Hobby :)
entry.content: You are an inspiration, Thank you for your great work.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM and SAP Enable Now present collaboration!
entry.content:
Hello Sinje,

Thank you for the blog.
Based on a very quick read, this seems to be similar to what has existed for Solman for some time, just that it comes to CALM. 

Is that correct?  Perhaps there's something more here or planned?

Best Regards, Wallace

entry.title: الصناعه
entry.content: مرحبا بالجميع أنا أبحث عن وثائق / معرف التدريب / شهادة على اللوجستيات الميدانية.أي مساعدة سيكون موضع تقدير.

entry.title: The completion date not editabl in the Fiori App EAMS_WDA_ORDNTF_OIF after SAP recomended customizi
entry.content: The completion date not present in the Fiori App EAMS_WDA_ORDNTF_OIF ; the completion field is present in the GUI app.   It is editable and in GUI based app but  it was not visible in the Webdynpro App. We raised a query with SAP support  and a recomendations was made to perform some customization for the Webdynpro . Now the fields are visible  and appear editable but do not retain any value if any value is entered in the completion date and time field.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM and SAP Enable Now present collaboration!
entry.content:
Yes .. I am not sure if it has exactly the same functionality, but the idea is the same.

and you won as the quickest response to a blog ever 😉

 

Cheers

Sinje

entry.title: Re: Upload data into SAP S/4HANA Cloud CBO from MS Excel
entry.content: is it also possible to delete the cbo data entries from excel. is it also possible to update the data?

entry.title: Re: In Signavio Process Manager,When will directory-link...
entry.content:
Hi there, 

Thank you for your feedback regarding the usage and consumption of value chains in our Value Accelerators. 

Unfortunately, SAP Signavio Process Manager currently does not have the ability to collectively remove dictionary links or selectively choose which to import.

I have provided this feedback to our Product and Engineering team as a suggestion in the product roadmap. 

Once again, thank you for the valuable feedback to improve our product!

entry.title: Re: I can't update the closedate field with dtw
entry.content: CloseDate is auto updated with system/current date when the production order status is changed to Closed. So it is not for user or DTW to input the date value.

entry.title: Re: Task 3 - Enhancing CDS View Entities with Annotations
entry.content:
Preview of the App showing all the columns, search and filter options in the CDS view entity:

 

Search records based on a specific value (busniess) from 'Description' field and select an available value (Accepted) from the 'Status' field.

entry.title: Re: From Monday Blues to Data Delights: My Geeky Hobby :)
entry.content: It’s impressive how you use your weekends to dive deeper into your craft, improving your skills and sharing your insights with others. The way you turn a busy week into something positive by learning, connecting with others, and attending seminars is truly motivating. Keep up the great work!

entry.title: Issue with long running PAL Function on HANA
entry.content:
We are facing an issue with a long running PAL Function (PAL Additive Model Forecast) on HANA.

The PAL function is called via AMDP script in an end routine of a BW transformation.
For 99% of our data the routine is running about 3 sec and everthing works fine, but for a specific subset of data the function is running 1 hour and then throws a time out on the scriptserver.

The function call looks like this:

 

call _SYS_AFL.PAL_ADDITIVE_MODEL_ANALYSIS(:lt_data, :holiday_tbl, :param_tbl, lt_model);

 

 lt_data:

FISCYEARSUM_ST
2018-01-0116
2019-01-015
2020-01-0133
2021-01-0111

lt_holiday_tbl: empty

param_tbl:

NAMEINT_VALUEDOUBLE_VALUESTRING_VALUE
GROWTH  linear

Active SQL-STATEMENTS on HANA DB:

 

call _SYS_AFL.PAL_ADDITIVE_MODEL_ANALYSIS("SYS"."_SYS_SS2_TMP_TABLE_119235207_LT_DATA_2_DA206DDCEB223042BE5C8EB168FEB187_3", "SYS"."_SYS_SS2_TMP_TABLE_119235207_HOLIDAY_TBL_2_DA206DDCEB223042BE5C8EB168FEB187_3", "SYS"."_SYS_SS2_TMP_TABLE_119235207_PARAM_TBL_2_DA206DDCEB223042BE5C8EB168FEB187_3", ?)
CALL "SAPHANADB"."/BIC/DP3UWF3KQQQ0HRQ1J6MG_A=>GLOBAL_END"('DTP_0WB0ZSP4QW51W5L6UF79IKB20', 'ODP_HANA', 'VCVP_AI_SALES_SHARE           ODP_HANA', 'DTASRC', 'EAXBPC04', 'ADSO', '20240822082208000034000', "SAPHANADB"."_SYS_SS2_TMP_TABLE_3_119235207_3367455460654702955_1", ?)
INSERT INTO "SAPHANADB"."/BIC/AEAXBPC041" ("RECORDMODE", "FISCYEAR", "PLANALG", "PLANMONTH", "PROD_SHARE", "SALESORG", "SUM_ST", "SUM_TOTAL", "ZVPBEPROD", "ZVPPLANKU" ,   "REQTSN" , "RECORD" , "DATAPAKID")  SELECT  "0RECORDMODE" "RECORDMODE",  "FISCYEAR" "FISCYEAR",  "PLANALG" "PLANALG",  "PLANMONTH" "PLANMONTH",  "PROD_SHARE" "PROD_SHARE",  "SALESORG" "SALESORG",  "SUM_ST" "SUM_ST",  "SUM_TOTAL" "SUM_TOTAL",  "ZVPBEPROD" "ZVPBEPROD",  "ZVPPLANKU" "ZVPPLANKU" ,'20240822082208000034000' AS "REQTSN",  ROW_NUMBER() OVER ( ORDER BY  "SALESORG", "#SOURCE#.4.SQL__PROCEDURE__SOURCE__RECORD" ) AS "RECORD" , '000001' AS "DATAPAKID" FROM "SAPHANADB"."/1BCAMDP/0BW:DAP:TR_0WB0ZSP4QW51W5L6UF79IKB20" ('PLACEHOLDER'=('$$DATUM$$.FORMULA','20240822'), 'PLACEHOLDER'=('TR_0WB0ZSP4QW51W5L6UF79IKB20.$$dtis_cross_join$$','"#DTISCROSS_ERROR" != ''X'''), 'PLACEHOLDER'=('TR_0WB0ZSP4QW51W5L6UF79IKB20.$$error_stack_semantic_join$$','"REQTSN" = ''20240822082208000034000'''), 'PLACEHOLDER'=('TR_0WB0ZSP4QW51W5L6UF79IKB20.$$filter$$','( ( "REQTSN" = ''20240822082208000034000'' ) )'), 'PLACEHOLDER'=('TR_0WB0ZSP4QW51W5L6UF79IKB20.$$i_error_handling$$','FALSE'), 'PLACEHOLDER'=('TR_0WB0ZSP4QW51W5L6UF79IKB20.$$i_req_dtp_name$$','DTP_0WB0ZSP4QW51W5L6UF79IKB20'), 'PLACEHOLDER'=('TR_0WB0ZSP4QW51W5L6UF79IKB20.$$i_req_logsys$$','ODP_HANA'), 'PLACEHOLDER'=('TR_0WB0ZSP4QW51W5L6UF79IKB20.$$i_req_requid$$','20240822082208000034000'), 'PLACEHOLDER'=('TR_0WB0ZSP4QW51W5L6UF79IKB20.$$i_req_src_name$$','VCVP_AI_SALES_SHARE           ODP_HANA'), 'PLACEHOLDER'=('TR_0WB0ZSP4QW51W5L6UF79IKB20.$$i_req_src_type$$','DTASRC'), 'PLACEHOLDER'=('TR_0WB0ZSP4QW51W5L6UF79IKB20.$$i_req_tgt_name$$','EAXBPC04'), 'PLACEHOLDER'=('TR_0WB0ZSP4QW51W5L6UF79IKB20.$$i_req_tgt_type$$','ADSO'), 'PLACEHOLDER'=('$$keydate$$','20240822'),'PLACEHOLDER' = ('ce_settings', '{"enable_partitioned_execution": "0" }')  )   ORDER BY "SALESORG", "#SOURCE#.4.SQL__PROCEDURE__SOURCE__RECORD"

 

 

BW-DTP (successful execution with other selection):

BW-DTP (time out on srciptserver):

scritpserver.log:

[17541]{349749}[9/967925937] 2024-08-22 11:22:17.000634 i TraceContext TraceContext.cpp(01367) : UserName=, ApplicationUserName=, ApplicationName=ABAP:, ApplicationSource=CL_SQL_STATEMENT==============CP:321, Client=111, StatementHash=4e5161bdd5d9c1209af3a9b2e13cf6f6, EppRootContextId=5D07CA1F0E4C4645A11D1F467F9B08F5, EppTransactionId=E04DBA5667800330E0066C67F643FB8C, EppConnectionId=2DEBC340605F11EF9CDFF8940A6ED1A2, EppConnectionCounter=14, EppComponentName=, EppAction=BIDTPR_20240822082208000034_1, StatementExecutionID=844437896413266

[17541]{349749}[9/967925937] 2024-08-22 11:22:17.000634 e AFL_PAL_COMMONFU PALObj.cpp(06096) : %%% [error: 73001066] [correlation id: 0xc03c810d100b0036] Error occurred while executing PAL function: PAL error[73001066]:Time out: the algorithm has executed with 3600 seconds.

[17541]{349749}[9/967925937] 2024-08-22 11:22:17.000674 E AFL_PAL_COMMONFU PALObj.cpp(06096) : [correlation id: 0xc03c810d100b0036] exception 1: no.73001066 (/data/jenkins_prod/workspace/all4qq0thb/s/AFL/PAL/palCommonFunction.cpp:35) TID: 17541

PAL error[73001066]:Time out: the algorithm has executed with 3600 seconds.

exception throw location:

0: 0x00007fc16348218b in AFL_PAL::TimeOutWatcher::assertTimeOut() const+0x57 at /data/jenkins_prod/workspace/all4qq0thb/s/AFL/PAL/palCommonFunction.cpp:35 (libaflpal.so)

1: 0x00007fc165216fb6 in double stan::model::log_prob_grad<true, false, AFL_PAL::pal_prophet::StanModelDecorator<prophet_model_namespace::prophet_model> >(AFL_PAL::pal_prophet::StanModelDecorator<prophet_model_namespace::prophet_model> const&, std::vector<double, std::allocator<double> >&, std::vector<int, std::allocator<int> >&, std::vector<double, std::allocator<double> >&, std::ostream*) [clone .isra.0]+0x1c2 at /data/jenkins_prod/workspace/all4qq0thb/s/AFL/PAL/TimeSeries/Prophet/PALProphet.cpp:36 (libaflpal.so)

2: 0x00007fc16525639c in stan::optimization::ModelAdaptor<AFL_PAL::pal_prophet::StanModelDecorator<prophet_model_namespace::prophet_model> >::operator()(Eigen::Matrix<double, -1, 1, 0, -1, 1> const&, double&, Eigen::Matrix<double, -1, 1, 0, -1, 1>&)+0x88 at /data/jenkins_prod/workspace/all4qq0thb/hm/c/dependencies/afl/stan-2.19.1-sap0-linuxx86_64/src/stan-2.19.1/src/stan/optimization/bfgs.hpp:354 (libaflpal.so)

3: 0x00007fc165256794 in int stan::optimization::WolfLSZoom<stan::optimization::ModelAdaptor<AFL_PAL::pal_prophet::StanModelDecorator<prophet_model_namespace::prophet_model> >, double, Eigen::Matrix<double, -1, 1, 0, -1, 1> >(double&, Eigen::Matrix<double, -1, 1, 0, -1, 1>&, double&, Eigen::Matrix<double, -1, 1, 0, -1, 1>&, stan::optimization::ModelAdaptor<AFL_PAL::pal_prophet::StanModelDecorator<prophet_model_namespace::prophet_model> >&, Eigen::Matrix<double, -1, 1, 0, -1, 1> const&, double const&, double const&, double const&, double const&, Eigen::Matrix<double, -1, 1, 0, -1, 1> const&, double, double, double, double, double, double, double const&) [clone .isra.0]+0x1b0 at /data/jenkins_prod/workspace/all4qq0thb/hm/c/dependencies/afl/stan-2.19.1-sap0-linuxx86_64/src/stan-2.19.1/src/stan/optimization/bfgs_linesearch.hpp:146 (libaflpal.so)

4: 0x00007fc165256ff9 in int stan::optimization::WolfeLineSearch<stan::optimization::ModelAdaptor<AFL_PAL::pal_prophet::StanModelDecorator<prophet_model_namespace::prophet_model> >, double, Eigen::Matrix<double, -1, 1, 0, -1, 1> >(stan::optimization::ModelAdaptor<AFL_PAL::pal_prophet::StanModelDecorator<prophet_model_namespace::prophet_model> >&, double&, Eigen::Matrix<double, -1, 1, 0, -1, 1>&, double&, Eigen::Matrix<double, -1, 1, 0, -1, 1>&, Eigen::Matrix<double, -1, 1, 0, -1, 1> const&, Eigen::Matrix<double, -1, 1, 0, -1, 1> const&, double const&, Eigen::Matrix<double, -1, 1, 0, -1, 1> const&, double const&, double const&, double const&, double const&, double const&)+0x3a5 at /data/jenkins_prod/workspace/all4qq0thb/hm/c/dependencies/afl/stan-2.19.1-sap0-linuxx86_64/src/stan-2.19.1/src/stan/optimization/bfgs_linesearch.hpp:283 (libaflpal.so)

5: 0x00007fc165257227 in stan::optimization::BFGSMinimizer<stan::optimization::ModelAdaptor<AFL_PAL::pal_prophet::StanModelDecorator<prophet_model_namespace::prophet_model> >, stan::optimization::LBFGSUpdate<double, -1>, double, -1>::step()+0x143 at /data/jenkins_prod/workspace/all4qq0thb/hm/c/dependencies/afl/stan-2.19.1-sap0-linuxx86_64/src/stan-2.19.1/src/stan/optimization/bfgs.hpp:205 (libaflpal.so)

6: 0x00007fc16525d1bc in int stan::services::optimize::lbfgs<AFL_PAL::pal_prophet::StanModelDecorator<prophet_model_namespace::prophet_model> >(AFL_PAL::pal_prophet::StanModelDecorator<prophet_model_namespace::prophet_model>&, stan::io::var_context&, unsigned int, unsigned int, double, int, double, double, double, double, double, double, int, bool, int, stan::callbacks::interrupt&, stan::callbacks::logger&, stan::callbacks::writer&, stan::callbacks::writer&) [clone .isra.0]+0x408 at /data/jenkins_prod/workspace/all4qq0thb/hm/c/dependencies/afl/stan-2.19.1-sap0-linuxx86_64/src/stan-2.19.1/src/stan/services/optimize/lbfgs.hpp:123 (libaflpal.so)

7: 0x00007fc16525f238 in void AFL_PAL::pal_stan::execute<AFL_PAL::pal_prophet::StanModelDecorator<prophet_model_namespace::prophet_model> >(AFL_PAL::pal_prophet::StanModelDecorator<prophet_model_namespace::prophet_model>&, stan::io::array_var_context&, AFL_PAL::pal_stan::stan_parameters&, AFL_PAL::pal_stan::stan_model&, std::vector<unsigned long, std::allocator<unsigned long> >&)+0x11a4 at /data/jenkins_prod/workspace/all4qq0thb/s/AFL/PAL/Utilities/StanCommon.hpp:156 (libaflpal.so)

8: 0x00007fc16521c34f in AFL_PAL::pal_prophet::PALProphet::execute()+0x261b at /data/jenkins_prod/workspace/all4qq0thb/s/AFL/PAL/Utilities/StanCommon.hpp:246 (libaflpal.so)

9: 0x00007fc16340e458 in CPAL::ADDITIVE_MODEL_ANALYSIS_ANY(ltt::smartptr_handle<TRexCommonObjects::InternalTable> const&, ltt::smartptr_handle<TRexCommonObjects::InternalTable> const&, ltt::smartptr_handle<TRexCommonObjects::InternalTable> const&, ltt::smartptr_handle<TRexCommonObjects::InternalTable>&) const+0x54 at /data/jenkins_prod/workspace/all4qq0thb/s/AFL/PAL/PALObj.cpp:6096 (libaflpal.so)

10: 0x00007fc163591e77 in ADDITIVE_MODEL_ANALYSIS_ANY__Wrapper+0x153 at AFL/PAL/PALWrapper.h:24572 (libaflpal.so)

11: 0x00007fc3116de508 in ljit::lib::AFLLibFunction::invoke(ljit::llang::LlangRuntimeContext*, void**, ljit::lib::AFLLibFunctionInfo const*)+0x24 at AFL/PackageManager/ComponentClass/AFLPM_ApplicationFunctionExecutor.hpp:118 (libhdbljitlib.so)

12: 0x00007fc213b7716c in ljit::dynamic/main-export+0x128 at afl/cen__SYS_AFL:_SYS_SS_CE_102741987_vers3_lang12_type1_[ FISCYEAR:TIMESTAMP SUM_ST:DECIMAL(18,3) ][ TS:TIMESTAMP HOLIDAY:VARCHAR(1) ][ NAME:VARCHAR(6) INT_VALUE:INT DOUBLE_VALUE:DOUBLE STRING_VALUE:VARCHAR(6) ]_140503528138752:3_TMP/3:0 (<unknown>)

13: 0x00007fc310589c68 in ljit::types::LlangManagedInvoke::invoke()+0xb4 at llvm/llangci/ljit_llangci_runtime.h:148 (libhdbljittypes1.so)

14: 0x00007fc30fdb4c88 in TrexCalculationEngine::ceLjitPop::executePopLlang(ltt_adp::vector<TrexCalculationEngine::ceDataSet*, ltt::integral_constant<bool, true> > const&, ltt_adp::vector<ltt::smartptr_handle<TRexCommonObjects::InternalTable>, ltt::integral_constant<bool, true> >&, ltt_adp::vector<ltt_adp::vector<TrexCalculationEngine::PlanViewAttribute, ltt::integral_constant<bool, true> >, ltt::integral_constant<bool, true> > const&, TRexCommonObjects::TRexApiError&, Executor::ExecutionInfo const&)+0x2724 at calculationEngine/pops/cePopCustomLjit.cpp:570 (libhdbcalcenginepops.so)

15: 0x00007fc30fdae2c3 in TrexCalculationEngine::ceLjitPop::executePopInternal(ltt_adp::vector<Executor::PlanData*, ltt::integral_constant<bool, true> > const&, ltt_adp::vector<Executor::PlanData*, ltt::integral_constant<bool, true> > const&, TRexCommonObjects::TRexApiError&, Executor::ExecutionInfo const&)+0x6e0 at calculationEngine/pops/cePopCustomLjit.cpp:136 (libhdbcalcenginepops.so)

16: 0x00007fc30fd55ac0 in TrexCalculationEngine::cePlanOperation::executePop(ltt_adp::vector<Executor::PlanData*, ltt::integral_constant<bool, true> > const&, ltt_adp::vector<Executor::PlanData*, ltt::integral_constant<bool, true> > const&, TRexCommonObjects::TRexApiError&, Executor::ExecutionInfo const&)+0x20 at calculationEngine/pops/cePopBase.cpp:1369 (libhdbcalcenginepops.so)

17: 0x00007fc3323d2d0e in Executor::X2::runPopTask(Executor::X2::PopTaskInfo&, int&, ltt::allocator&, ltt::allocator&)+0x2bea at executor/X2.cpp:2874 (libhdbexecutor.so)

18: 0x00007fc3323c8550 in Executor::X2::runPopJob(Executor::X2Job*)+0x70 at ltt/ext/hnd/releasable_handle.hpp:444 (libhdbexecutor.so)

19: 0x00007fc3323e1ba7 in Executor::X2Job::run(Execution::Context&, Execution::JobObject&)+0x423 at executor/X2.cpp:5954 (libhdbexecutor.so)

20: 0x00007fc31424ec0b in Execution::JobObjectImpl::run(Execution::JobWorker*)+0x13d7 at Basis/Execution/impl/JobExecutorRunJob.cpp:772 (libhdbbasis.so)

21: 0x00007fc31425658a in Execution::JobWorker::runJob(ltt::smartptr_handle<Execution::JobObjectForHandle>&)+0x8a6 at Basis/Execution/impl/JobExecutorThreads.cpp:379 (libhdbbasis.so)

22: 0x00007fc314258278 in Execution::JobWorker::run(Execution::ThreadRC&)+0x744 at Basis/Execution/impl/JobExecutorThreads.cpp:1382 (libhdbbasis.so)

23: 0x00007fc314298fd4 in Execution::Thread::staticMainImp(Execution::Thread*)+0x610 at Basis/Execution/impl/Thread.cpp:612 (libhdbbasis.so)

24: 0x00007fc31429f5bf in Execution::pthreadFunctionWrapper(Execution::Thread*)+0x1eb at Basis/Execution/impl/ThreadInterposition.cpp:684 (libhdbbasis.so)

25: 0x00007fc313c5d6ea in start_thread+0xd8 (libpthread.so.0)

26: 0x00007fc31358458f in __GI___clone+0x3d (libc.so.6)

exception type information:

- 0: public ltt::exception@0x00007fc313b34290

[17541]{349749}[9/967925937] 2024-08-22 11:22:17.025428 e ceExecute cePopCustomLjit.cpp(00580) : Llang Runtime Error: Exception::SQLException423: exception 73001066: PAL error[73001066]:Time out: the algorithm has executed with 3600 seconds.

 

at main (line 4) (_SYS_AFL.AFLPAL:ADDITIVE_MODEL_ANALYSIS_ANY:)

[17541]{349749}[9/967925937] 2024-08-22 11:22:17.025444 e ceExecute cePopCustomLjit.cpp(00605) : _SYS_AFL.AFLPAL:ADDITIVE_MODEL_ANALYSIS_ANY: [423] (range 3) AFL error exception: exception 73001066: PAL error[73001066]:Time out: the algorithm has executed with 3600 seconds.

 

Has anyone faced a similar issue?
Can anyone explain why this happens or has an advice how to avoid the long runtime of the function?

entry.title: RAP- Value help based on another field input
entry.content:
Hi,

I have a requirement to show value help for 2 fields which are dependent. Second field value help should be based on the first field.

For example, Material and suppliers. Based on the material selected, value help should be  shown for supplier and vice-versa if possible.

I have tried additional binding with usage: #FILTER  but it is not helping to get the results. Parameter CDS Views are not supported in the value help. Please guide me with any possibilities for the solution.

Thanks,

Liya

entry.title: Re: locked for automatic sourcing
entry.content: I could able to create the production order with the status locked for automatic sourcing in production version by changing the OPL8 configurations

entry.title: Course assignments from different workareas
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm planning to do some workarea housekeeping, and want to create a new workarea and move some existing content across. 

Will this cause problems in the future for existing users who have completed course assignments via the original workarea in so much as if they are a assigned a learning object they've previously completed it won't recognise the previous completion? 

I guess that's a long way of me asking is there a way to define learning object equivalencies? 

Many Thanks,

Andrew

entry.title: Re: CLM in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi JFPC, 
Do you know if CLM can be used with FICA?

entry.title: Re: Vendor Master Workflow is not triggered successfully
entry.content:
I did trace the event and found the event was created. Both of them looks same but one of them failed to get VM items.

Below is success WF.

Below is failed WF.

I'm not sure why one of the WF failed to get VM item as shown in the graphical workflow log. 

Please teach me how to troubleshoot the error.

entry.title: How to make field optional to mandatory in Import Sales Order Upload Template?
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 

The Sales Order Upload template used in Import Sales Order App under SAP Cloud Public Edition, I want to make Reference SD Document field as mandatory field which is currently optional, is it possible & how to do the same, prompt solutions will be highly appreciable

Thanks

Rahul Singhal

entry.title: Navigating the Nucleus of SAP FI: A Guide to Organizational Units and Essential Configurations - 1
entry.content:
Welcome to the first installment of our SAP FI learning series!

Hello, fellow SAP enthusiasts! As I embark on my journey to learn SAP Financial Accounting (FI), I thought it would be a fantastic opportunity to share my discoveries and insights with you through this blog. My goal is to not only enhance my own understanding but also to create a collaborative learning environment where we can grow together. In this initial post, we'll be exploring the Organizational Units and Basic Settings in SAP FI. I encourage you to contribute to this journey with your comments, questions, and suggestions. Let's dive in and demystify SAP FI together!

1. What is the Enterprise Structure in SAP?

The enterprise structure in SAP is a hierarchical framework that represents the varied and complex business entities and their relationships within an organization. It is the backbone for configuration and allows the SAP system to reflect the real-world business scenarios and reporting requirements of a company. 

In its standard version, SAP provides a set of predefined organizational units to represent the typical structure of a business in areas such as Human Resources, Logistics, and Accounting. These units serve as templates that you can use as a starting point for configuring your SAP system. They include:

  • Company Code: A sample legal entity for financial reporting.
  • Plant: A default location for manufacturing or service provision.
  • Sales Organization: A basic structure for handling sales processes.
  • Purchase Organization: A foundational unit for managing procurement activities.

Matching SAP's Structure with Business Requirements:
While SAP's standard enterprise structure is a valuable starting point, it is just that—a starting point. Each business has its own complexities and specific requirements that may not be fully addressed by SAP's samples. It's a good practice to attempt to match the structure provided by SAP to your specific requirements as closely as possible.

2. What is Organizational Units?

Organizational Units in SAP are structures that define the different segments of a business in the SAP environment. They are designed to represent the legal and organizational views of a company and are used to map a business's operational framework within the SAP system. Organizational Units serve as the backbone for configuration, enabling the system to handle a wide array of business processes and allowing for detailed reporting and management.

Key Types of SAP Organizational Units:
The various modules in SAP, such as Financial Accounting (FI), Controlling (CO), Sales and Distribution (SD), Material Management (MM), and Human Resources (HR), all utilize specific Organizational Units to represent different aspects of the business. Some of the key Organizational Units you will encounter in SAP include:

  1. Client: The highest hierarchical level in an SAP system that represents the entire enterprise and holds data that is shared across the organization.
  2. Company Code: A legal entity for which a complete set of accounts is maintained for external reporting purposes, including profit, loss, and balance sheet reports.
  3. Plant: In logistics and manufacturing, a plant is an operational unit where goods are produced or services provided.
  4. Storage Location: A physical or logical division within a plant where materials are kept.
  5. Sales Organization: The unit responsible for distributing goods and services, negotiating sales conditions, and carrying out business transactions.
  6. Purchase Organization: The unit that procures materials and services, negotiates terms and conditions with vendors, and is responsible for such transactions.
  7. Controlling Area: An Organizational Unit within management accounting that represents a closed system for cost accounting purposes, often aligned with a company code or multiple company codes.

 

3. What is Company?

A 'Company' in SAP FI is an organizational entity that represents an enterprise. It is typically used to consolidate financial statements across multiple company codes. Here are some key attributes of a 'Company' in SAP:

  1. Alphanumeric Representation: A 'Company' is identified in the SAP system by a unique six-digit alphanumeric code. This code is used to differentiate between different 'Companies' within the same SAP environment, especially in organizations with multiple lines of business or subsidiaries.
  2. Optional Creation: Setting up a 'Company' in the SAP system is an optional step. Organizations may decide to create a 'Company' when they need to consolidate financial statements across several Company Codes. However, if the business does not require consolidated reporting at a group level, they may opt not to define a 'Company'.
  3. Inclusion of Company Codes: A 'Company' can encompass one or more Company Codes. This allows for the consolidation of financial data from various legal entities, each represented by its own Company Code, into a single group report.
  4. Transaction Code for Creation: The transaction code to create a 'Company' in SAP FI is OX15. This transaction code is used to access the screen where you can define the attributes of the 'Company', such as its name, alphanumeric code, and address details.

 

4. What is Company Code?

The Company Code in SAP FI is a fundamental building block, represented by a unique four-character alphanumeric identifier. This identifier is the primary means by which the system distinguishes between different legal entities. Let's delve into the specifics:

  1. Four-Character Identification: Each Company Code is uniquely identified by a four-character alphanumeric code, which is tailored to an organization’s internal naming conventions and helps in organizing financial data effectively.
  2. Mandatory Creation: Unlike some organizational units in SAP, the creation of at least one Company Code per client is not just a recommendation; it is mandatory. Without a Company Code, it is impossible to execute financial transactions or generate financial statements in SAP FI.
  3. Minimum Requirement: At a minimum, one Company Code must be defined within each client to implement the SAP FI module. This is the primary requirement for setting up financial accounting processes and integrating with other modules.
  4. Multiple Company Codes: While at least one Company Code is required, SAP allows for the definition of multiple Company Codes within the same client. This enables businesses with multiple legal entities to manage their financial transactions separately while operating under the same SAP client.
  5. Methods of Creation: Creating a Company Code can be approached in two ways: by copying from an existing Company Code or by creating a new one from scratch. Copying an existing Company Code is often faster and ensures consistency in configuration settings, while creating one from scratch allows for a more tailored setup.
  6. Transaction Code for Creation: The transaction code to create a Company Code in SAP FI is OX02. This transaction brings you to the initial screen where you can either define a new Company Code or copy from an existing one, setting the stage for all subsequent financial activities within that entity.

 

 
 

Conclusion:

As we've just scratched the surface of SAP FI with Organizational Units and Basic Settings, I hope you've gained a clearer understanding of these essential concepts. Remember, this is a learning process for all of us, and I'm learning right alongside you. In our upcoming posts, we will delve deeper into other SAP FI components such as the General Ledger, Accounts Payable, and Accounts Receivable. Your feedback and insights are invaluable to this shared learning experience, so please feel free to leave comments, ask questions, or suggest topics you'd like to see covered. Together, let's unlock the full potential of SAP FI!

Stay tuned for our next installment, where we'll explore the intricacies of the General Ledger in SAP FI. Happy learning, and let's support each other on this educational adventure!

entry.title: Re: Deleted Payment Draft Change Log

entry.content:
@LiyaAjayan 

You can find a similar question here. If the mandatory (key) fields are filled in the background, the Object Page directly opens without showing a dialog.

entry.title: Re: RAP-Create option: Can all fields including key can b...
entry.content:
Thank you.

I have already checked this link. So, if we have key fields, then it will show in separate dialog. No options to change the behaviour?

entry.title: Determine class structure / components (local class, test classes, ...)
entry.content:
Hello,

I am looking for a solution to determine the structure / components of a class.

Regarding "structure and/or components" I mean for example local classes or test classes of a global class!
I do NOT want to analyze attributes or methods by using a type descriptor.

So I guess that tables exist where such information CCAU, CCDEF etc. are stored...

Can anyone give me a hint, in which tables such information is stored?
I would start retrieving the class name of a global class by reading table TADIR.

Maybe there is a "static" variant of a solution by reading several tables or a "dynamic" variant by using any kind of RTTI?

BR

entry.title: Re: Extending Fiori Elements, make a field read-only
entry.content:
Hi,

have you tried this approach - adding a change file with similar contents:

{
    "changeType": "propertyChange",
    "reference": "app.variant1",
    "namespace": "apps/app.variant1/changes/",
    "projectId": "app.variant1",
    "packageName": "$TMP",
    "support": {
        "generator": "sap.ui.rta.command",
        "command": "property",
        "sapui5Version": "1.129.0-SNAPSHOT"
    },
    "originalLanguage": "EN",
    "layer": "VENDOR",
    "fileType": "change",
    "fileName": "id_1724312853700_249_propertyChange",
    "content": {
        "property": "editable",
        "newValue": false
    },
    "texts": {},
    "selector": {
        "id": "cus.sd.prebillingdoc.creates1::sap.suite.ui.generic.template.ListReport.view.ListReport::C_PrelimBillingDueListItem--InvoiceDatePicker",
        "type": "sap.m.DatePicker",
        "idIsLocal": false
    },
    "dependentSelector": {},
    "jsOnly": false,
    "creation": "2024-08-22T07:48:37.006Z"
}
 
Regards,
Hristo

entry.title: Re: Introducing SAPUI5 Adaptation Projects in Visual Studio Code

  • SAP reentrance tickets - it could not open the url because the server unexpectedly dropped the connection.

Is it because I didn't login the cloud foundry? For some reason my BTP account didn't provide cf api.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Introducing SAP Analytics Cloud, add-in for Microsoft PowerPoint
entry.content:
Hi @Ashok_R,

Great blog and very appreciated functionality in SAC! It seems that data refresh is a bit buggy. Refresh itself works but the look of the graph doesn't look good afterwards. Here is my experience:

Inserting the widget looks ok:

But the look changes completely after data refresh:

Beside that graphs get blurry then, too. I guess there is room for improvement, isn't it?

Can you have a look into the data refresh feature, please?

Many thanks in advance!

Best regards

Lothar

entry.title: I'm trying to extract a report from custom code analytics
entry.content:
Hi SAP!

I'm trying to extract a report from custom code analytics in the custom code objects usage by type section. Could you help me?

Thank you.

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: I'm trying to extract a report from custom code analy...
entry.content:
Custom Code Analytics is SAP ONE Support Launchpad application. The application uses aggregated custom code data from your SAP EarlyWatch Alert data from your SAP S/4HANA environment to provide an automated, fact-based dashboard containing the most critical data related to your custom code, and will highlight & recommend potential improvement offerings that can be utilized in order to take action on the current situation, to improve the overall custom code footprint.

The application gives an overview about the custom code footprint of your ABAP systems and provides insights about code quality and usage.

You can click on - "Custom Code Usage by Object Type" header/title which is in the top of the unused graph section. This will show Custom Code Usage by Object Type table with additional details.

For more information regarding Custom Code Analytics please refer to the following documents:

2765131 - Getting Started with Custom Code Analytics based on SAP Solution Manager https://me.sap.com/notes/2765131

3483840 - Custom Code Analytics tiles explanation https://me.sap.com/notes/3483840

Custom Code Analytics - One tool for complete cust... - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: Fixed Deposit gets doubled in Investment / Increase after rollover
entry.content:
Hi Harry,

Which version of SAP you are using? I also faced such issues with final repayment after rollovers in 1909 version, i looked for SAP notes that time and it worked for me.

In my case final repayment itself was gone and i used 2975334. 

Regards,

Rohit

entry.title: Transport Custom Workflow from development to quality server
entry.content:
Hello,

How can I transport a simple custom workflow (without custom objects or tasks) from dev to qa?
Can this be done only with se10?
I have to transport multiple workflows so re creating it in the quality server is not possible.
As far as now I cannot find the workflow I ve transfered in QA,
in swdd or in the ws task display transaction. Like the workflow was never transported, only the event linkage of it.

Thank you

entry.title: The Strategic Importance of Procurement in Enterprise Companies
entry.content:
Introduction

Imagine a thriving company suddenly losing millions because one key supplier failed to deliver critical components on time. This scenario isn't just hypothetical—it’s a harsh reality faced by many businesses. Inefficiencies in procurement can cost companies up to 20% of their annual revenue, underscoring how crucial this function is to a company's success.

Enterprise companies, with their extensive product lines, large scale, global customer bases, and intense competition, operate in highly complex markets where effective procurement processes are paramount.

This essay explores the strategic importance of procurement, delving into its role as a core component of any enterprise's operational structure.

Purpose

This essay aims to elucidate the crucial role procurement plays in large companies by examining how it integrates within organizational frameworks, impacts performance, collaborates with other departments, and utilizes technology.

Understanding these aspects will provide insight into why intelligent procurement strategies are fundamental to business success and how they can be optimized for better outcomes.

The Strategic Importance of Procurement

Procurement isn't just about buying things; it’s a vital link in the value chain connecting companies with their customers, suppliers, and other stakeholders. It ensures the smooth flow of materials, information, and money, acting as a bridge between the inner workings of the company and the outside world.

For instance, a major player in the automotive industry needs to source thousands of parts from a global network of suppliers to assemble their vehicles. By strategically aligning their procurement process, they maintain high production rates, ensure quality control, and manage costs efficiently. This alignment not only secures a steady supply chain but also enables the company to react swiftly to market changes and technological advancements, providing a competitive edge. Procurement supports essential functions like product development, marketing, sales, operations, and finance. When managed effectively, it aligns sourcing decisions with business goals, capitalizes on market opportunities, and creates competitive advantages.

The Role of Procurement in a Large Enterprise from a Strategic Perspective

From a strategic viewpoint, procurement helps the company achieve its vision, mission, and goals by:

Understanding Business Strategy:

Analyzing internal and external environments, strengths, and stakeholder expectations.

Let us use a pharmaceutical giant as an example, they invest in market research to understand global health trends and regulatory changes, allowing the procurement team to anticipate needs and align sourcing strategies proactively.

Developing Sourcing Strategy:

Defining objectives, evaluating suppliers, negotiating deals, and managing sourcing activities. The company establishes a strategic sourcing group to identify and evaluate suppliers based on criteria such as quality, cost, and ethical practices.

By forming strategic partnerships with specialized suppliers, the company secures vital resources for groundbreaking drug development.

Aligning Sourcing with Business Strategy:

Ensuring sourcing decisions support business goals and create synergies. Aligning procurement goals with a mission to deliver affordable healthcare solutions, the company negotiates long-term agreements to hedge against raw material price volatility, ensuring cost consistency without compromising quality or ethical standards.

Supporting Overall Strategy:

Achieving goals like market share growth and enhanced customer loyalty.

The pharmaceutical company sources from sustainable and ethical suppliers to meet its corporate social responsibility commitments. This approach enhances its brand reputation among consumers and investors, ensures compliance with industry regulations, and fosters customer loyalty.

Through these strategic initiatives, the procurement function significantly contributes to the company's overall strategy, ensuring a robust, efficient supply chain aligned with long-term goals. This seamless integration aids in achieving market growth, innovation, and customer satisfaction.

Organizational Placement of Procurement

Where procurement fits in a company can vary widely based on factors like centralization, integration, and responsibilities. These factors determine whether procurement decisions are centralized, decentralized, hybrid, matrix, or networked.

Consider a large technology firm navigating a complex global supply chain. Initially, it implemented a decentralized procurement function to grant autonomy to regional offices, allowing rapid decision-making and responsiveness to local market conditions.

However, inconsistencies in supplier quality and contractual terms increased costs and risks.

In response, the firm transitioned to a hybrid procurement model. While regional offices retained some autonomy, key procurement decisions, such as supplier selection and major contract negotiations, were centralized at headquarters. This shift leveraged the benefits of centralized control—such as standardized processes, bulk purchasing, and compliance—without sacrificing the agility offered by decentralization.

Here's a breakdown of different organizational placement strategies and their respective pros and cons:

Centralized Function: Offers control and efficiency but may lack flexibility and innovation.

Decentralized Function: Provides autonomy and agility but can lead to complexity and inconsistency.

Hybrid Function: Balances centralization and decentralization but may create confusion.

Matrix Function: Enhances collaboration but can cause ambiguity and tensions.

Network Function: Leverages external expertise and resources but comes with risks.

Through their hybrid model, the technology firm achieved a more cohesive and efficient procurement process, resulting in lower costs, standardized supplier quality, and better alignment with corporate goals.

Impact of Procurement on Enterprise Performance

Procurement significantly affects a company’s performance, profitability, and sustainability:

Cost Reduction: Better prices, consolidated purchases, and optimized inventory lower overall costs.

For example, by negotiating multi-year contracts with key suppliers, the manufacturer secures bulk discounts and stabilizes pricing, significantly reducing expenditure and enhancing budget predictability.

Quality Improvement: Choosing reliable suppliers and adhering to quality standards improve product and service quality. Rigorous supplier audits and quality checks result in high-quality components that enhance product performance and durability, elevating customer satisfaction and brand reputation.

Risk Mitigation: Conducting thorough checks and diversifying supply sources manage and minimize risks. By diversifying its supply base across multiple geographies, the manufacturer mitigates risks associated with geopolitical instability, natural disasters, and supplier insolvency. This approach ensures a resilient supply chain capable of withstanding disruptions.

Driving Innovation: Finding new technologies and collaborating with suppliers fosters innovation. Collaborating with cutting-edge tech firms and startups, the company integrates groundbreaking technologies such as Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Internet of Things (IoT) into their products, maintaining a competitive edge in a fast-evolving market.

Creating Value: Aligning sourcing strategies with business goals creates and delivers value. By sourcing sustainable materials and engaging in ethical procurement practices, the company meets regulatory requirements and enhances its corporate social responsibility profile, appealing to eco-conscious consumers and investors.

Through strategic procurement practices, the manufacturer transforms procurement from a cost center into a dynamic element of competitive advantage, bolstering overall enterprise performance and sustainability.

Conclusion

Procurement is essential for enterprise companies, influencing strategic direction, operational efficiency, and value creation.

Its successful integration and effectiveness depend on factors like centralization and collaboration. Smart procurement can reduce costs, improve quality, mitigate risks, drive innovation, and create value.

Recall the scenario of the thriving company losing millions due to a key supplier's failure. If that company had optimized its procurement processes, it could have avoided such a catastrophic loss. By investing in advanced technologies, fostering collaboration between departments, and aligning procurement strategies with business goals, companies can navigate complex markets and achieve lasting success.

Industry leaders and stakeholders must recognize the strategic importance of procurement and take proactive steps to enhance its role within their organizations. Strengthening your procurement strategies today will secure your business’s future, preventing you from being the next example of inefficiencies.

If you wish to view this in a video format, please go to https://sapvideo.cfapps.eu10-004.hana.ondemand.com/?entry_id=1_we7gk2nm

Lars

entry.title: How to configure multi dunning procedure to dun the consumers separate percentage if fails to pay
entry.content:
Need advice from ISU FI-CA functional consultants. We have currently single dunning level where we charged consumers straight 10% of electricity charges if fails to pay within the pre-configured days (for example 14 factory calendar days for regular consumers). Now we have requirement that we need to charge 5% of electricity charges as late payment surcharge if consumer paid after due date within 3 days and after 3 more days (i.e. 17 days), charge 5% of electricity charges (total 10% of initial posted electricity charges).

How can we cater this requirement from Payment terms (or terms of payment), or dunning procedure, or dunning level.

entry.title: Issue with BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE: Error Message Related to Incorrect Material Reference
entry.content:
Hello,

I received the error message "Of the required quantity of material &1, $2 PC remain(s) open" while using BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE. This occurred during a 101 GR for a subcontracting PO. I understand that this message typically appears when the vendor's $1 stock is insufficient.

However, in this case, the $1 material mentioned in the message is not the subcontracted material. Additionally, there is no material provision value in the BOM, and the material does not exist in the PO components either.

I would appreciate your assistance in finding the cause of this issue.

Thank you very much for your help.

Best regards,

entry.title: Problem of copying text from service order to billing document item level texts
entry.content: We encountered an issue when displaying the invoice form from a service order. The problem is as follows: after making a text note at the item and header level of the service order, this note (message) cannot be transferred to the invoice. Since it doesn’t carry over, we are unable to display it on the invoice form. Has anyone faced a similar issue, and what would you suggest as a solution? Thank you.

entry.title: Re: How to get max date of intervention request
entry.content: Can anyone help with this please? I just need the most recent session request date to put on a report. Thanks

Thanks for getting back to me and also for passing this on to the Product Team, as I believe this can be a relevant feature in order to better leverage the VAL diagrams.

Kind Regards.

JD

entry.title: Re: Task 3 - Enhancing CDS View Entities with Annotations
entry.content:
My solution:

  • All the findings:

  • Search by Description & Status:

entry.title: Contracts Redesign in SAP for Me for Partners
entry.content:
SAP is committed to improving your experience as a partner. As a result, we have made changes on how you can manage your customers contracts and renewals in SAP for Me.

If you are a direct customer of SAP, you can find blog posts here on the new functionalities available for customers:

  • Explore the Newly Redesigned Cloud Card Tab! 
  • New Self-Service Cloud Digital Terminations and Retraction Features now Available in SAP for Me!

As an SAP Partner, you can view your customer contracts in the Customer Success Dashboard, on the Contracts tab.

Navigate to the Cloud Active Sales Contracts or On-Premise Active Sales Contracts card, and click on the blue hyperlinked Customer Name to view a full 360 degree view of that customer.

Cloud Customer

If you click on a customer in the Cloud Active Sales Contracts card, you will be brought to the Customer Details Dashboard for that customer, on the Cloud Contracts tab.

There you will find a new card which consolidates two previous tabs (Portfolio and Orders & Renewals).

Key Features of the New Cloud Contracts Card:

Unified View: The Contracts card under the Cloud tab now consolidates all Customer | Partner cloud contracts, providing visibility to expiring contracts at the top, sorted by end date.

Retained Functionalities: Enjoy the same popular features such as search functionality, contract expiry notifications, and direct access to contract details.

Enhanced Capabilities: Users can customize their view by filtering, sorting, grouping, and adjusting columns as per their needs. 

View your contracts and orders for this customer listed here, including the Solution Area, Materials, Contract End Date, Days to renewal, and Renewal Type.

You can customize the table using the buttons in the header to sort, filter, group, search or export the contents.

Click the gear icon in the top right top right header to add or remove columns, including Deal Type.

Tick the column headers you want to add and click OK. Now it will be shown in the card. 

For example, in the screenshot below you can see the Deal Model Column is now appearing.

Click the star icon to favorite the contract. Your favorite items will then appear in the Favorites card on your homepage.

Click Manage PO Numbers to review your Purchase Order Numbers and submit a new Purchase Order.

The new Manage Contract menu allows you to review your contract and make changes:

  • Open Contract Details: View further details on the specific contract, including addresses and terms
  • Get More Licenses: Order additional licenses via the SAP Store (Resell only)
  • Terminate Contract: Cancel the contract (Resell, PMC/BPO and SAP Store only)

Select the reason for termination and click Submit Request.

You can monitor the status of the request on the Contract Details Page. If you need to retract your termination request, you can do this. For more information on terminations and retractions, including contracts in scope, please see this post: New Self-Service Cloud Digital Termination and Ret... - SAP Community

  • Get assistance: Request support regarding your PO or invoice

     

On-Premise Customer

If you click on a customer in the on-Premise Active Sales Contracts card, you will be brought to the Customer Details Dashboard for that customer, on the On-Premise Contracts tab.

There, the pre-existing Orders, Materials and On-Premise Maintenance cards have been consolidated.

If required, you can Manage PO Numbers in the On-Premise Maintenance card. Learn more here: Purchase Order Number Submissions for Partners now... - SAP Community

 

Required Authorization

Contact the Partner Users & Administration Manager in your company or Request authorization via the card. 

To view contracts:

  • End Customer Info & Support

To manage contracts (PO collection, Terminate Contract, Get More Licenses):

  • End Customer Ordering

entry.title: Re: Task 3 - Enhancing CDS View Entities with Annotations
entry.content: Here is my submission.

entry.title: Help Needed: t_stpo-mat_provis Not Updating in BOM Using 'CSAP_MAT_BOM_MAINTAIN'
entry.content:
Hello,

I am currently using the BAPI 'CSAP_MAT_BOM_MAINTAIN' to update a BOM. However, when adding values to the BOM, the value in t_stpo-mat_provis does not seem to be reflected in the BOM.

 

The logic I am using is as follows:

CALL FUNCTION 'CSAP_MAT_BOM_MAINTAIN'
        EXPORTING
          material           = material
          plant              = plant
          bom_usage          = bom_usage
*         ALTERNATIVE        =
*         VALID_FROM         =
          change_no          = change_no
*         REVISION_LEVEL     =
          i_stko             = i_stko
*         FL_NO_CHANGE_DOC   = ' '
          fl_commit_and_wait = 'X'
*         FL_CAD             = ' '
          fl_bom_create      = 'X'
          fl_new_item        = 'X'
*         FL_COMPLETE        = ' '
*         FL_DEFAULT_VALUES  = 'X'
*         FL_IDENTIFY_BY_GUID       = ' '
*         FL_RECURSIVE       = ' '
        IMPORTING
          fl_warning         = fl_warning
          o_stko             = o_stko
        TABLES
          t_stpo             = t_stpo
*         T_DEP_DATA         =
*         T_DEP_DESCR        =
*         T_DEP_ORDER        =
*         T_DEP_SOURCE       =
*         T_DEP_DOC          =
*         T_DOC_LINK         =
*         T_DMU_TMX          =
*         T_LTX_LINE         =
          t_stpu             = t_stpu
*         T_FSH_BOMD         =
*         T_SGT_BOMC         =
        EXCEPTIONS
          error              = 1
          OTHERS             = 2.

Is there anything I might be missing or any additional parameters that I should include?

I would greatly appreciate your assistance.

Thank you very much for your help.

Best regards,

entry.title: Global Assignment Automatic Timeslice Creation Issue
entry.content:
During actions Add Global Assignment or End Global Assignment, an automatic timeslice is created on the dependents portlet even before the workflow is approved. This is causing a duplicate timeslice issue when approving the workflow since the similar workflow will also trigger a timeslice creation in the same portlet, which causes an error and blocks the workflow approval.

Are there other customers experiencing the same issue?

I have raised the below influence request to improve the behavior during GA actions to avoid manual deletion of the automatically created timeslice to make way for the one included in the workflow.

Improvement Request Details - Customer Influence (sap.com)

entry.title: Re: Issue with shift and shift interval transfer from S4H...
entry.content:
Hi Gizem,

from the 2408 release note: https://me.sap.com/notes/3477097 

"When you download work centers from integrated ERP systems, SAP Digital Manufacturing creates resources from capacities assigned to the work centers. SAP Digital Manufacturing then automatically creates and assigns shifts to the resources. From release 2408, SAP Digital Manufacturing no longer automatically assigns shifts to downloaded resources. We recommend that you check your shift definitions and then assign shifts manually."

Could this be the cause of the issues you're facing?

Best,

Mike

Hello Daniel,

i am facing the same issue, please let us know if you have solved it and how. 

 

thanks.

entry.title: Re: Unable to create a Remote Source from Hana database (...
entry.content: Hello mamartins, many thanks for your answer! I think your suggestion is for ODBC connection. And we are using the MssqlLogReaderAdapter. Do we have to use a different adapter ? Is our SQL statement for creating the remote source correct ? Many thanks!

entry.title: Add CAP service to an existing Product including multitenancy to ensure CMK
entry.content:
Hey colleagues,

I have a specific question regarding the integration of a CAP service into an existing product.
We have a service which customers can use via BTP. It is not a subscription SaaS application. The customer adds the entitlement to any subaccount and afterwards create an instance in the desired spaces. So far the service contains multiple workers. However, the customer only get access to the API worker. The others are connected via a Message Queue.
We received new requirements which lead us to the decision to create a new service in our solution. We want to build this service as a CAP service. It should be called internally by our workers but also externally by the customers. Mainly we store information in the service and the customer can GET the information.

Now we came across the question how we can handle multitenancy inside of the CAP service to also enable the CMK feature for customers. First there is no subscription as we would deploy the image within our pipeline when a customers uses our service. Second I don't see a way how we can store information on behalf of the customer to encrypt it in the correct way.

Can you guide us into the right direction?
The attachment sketches the highlivel system overview.

entry.content:
Hello Qu-LUI

we have a problem here as we have deleted purchase order after GR is created but still delivery indicator is visible even after deleting po lineitems how to remove delivery indicator here in this case

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA APIs and Where to Find Them
entry.content:
Hi Thomas,

another remark - what do you think about report ABAP_DOCU_RELEASED_APIS instead of going to the git repository? We found this in the help documentation: ABAP Keyword Documentation (sap.com)

Best regards,

Lisa

entry.title: Story reports Time type end date filter issues
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have a question regarding Story reports, I created a query and report to fetch employee absence data.

Problem is when I have absence example from dd-mm: 25.7. to 4.8.

Report should catch only data for 7. month , and show absences for that month, which is for this 7. month total 4 days, but instead it is giving us the whole absence days count from 25.7. to 4.8. which is 7 days in total.

I tried with filter end date 31.7. but then it doesnt show this absence at all since its end date is 4.8.

Any advices how to resolve this?

Thanks,

entry.title: Re: Issue with shift and shift interval transfer from S4H to DM
entry.content: Hello,

entry.title: #06 - Utilize SAP Best Practices: Maximizing Your Project Success wit SAP Signavio Value Accelerator
entry.content:
Stay tuned—our blog is set to launch on November 12nd, 2024!

entry.title: Re: Issue with shift and shift interval transfer from S4H...
entry.content:
Hello,

The SAP DM shift integration with ERP does not download every aspect yet. But it is planned to be supported in future. From 2408, the WC download will get the shift downloaded, but will not get assigned with the Resources automatically. We recommend you check the 2408 notes, and you can use the DM local shift maintenance until the complete shift integration is available.

BR,
Sumanta

entry.title: Re: Issue with long running PAL Function on HANA
entry.content:
Is it also running to a timeout when you call the procedure directly from the database?

DO BEGIN 
lt_data = <fill your data from lt_data>; 
lt_holiday = <provide the empty lt_holiday table>; 
lt_param = <fill the table from param_tbl>;
CALL _SYS_AFL.PAL_ADDITIVE_MODEL_ANALYSIS (:lt_data, :lt_holiday, :lt_param, lt_model); 
INSERT INTO PAL_ADDITIVE_MODEL_ANALYSIS_MODEL SELECT * FROM :lt_model; 
END; 
SELECT * FROM PAL_ADDITIVE_MODEL_ANALYSIS_MODEL;

entry.title: How should the cost center key be defined in Employee Central?
entry.content:
Dear All,

According to the following SAP Help documentation, the SAP recommendation is to create the cost center with Controlling Area Key and Cost Center Key in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central:

  • SAP Help - Replicating Cost Centers from SAP S/4HANA to Employee Central -> Cost Center Key Mapping
  • SAP Help - Integrating SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central with SAP Master Data Integration -> Configuring the Integration Object

What are the reasons against the SAP recommendation?

Thanks for the answers and best regards,
Andy

entry.title: Resource scheduling app- Order operation is confirmed but in scheduling still showing not completed
entry.content:
Hi 

In Scheduling its showing "not completed" & " Completion in Addition"  even after order operation confirmation done. 

Scheduling is done between 18-08-2024 & 24-08-2024, Confirmation is done on 21-08-2024 , 

 

 S/4 HANA 2021 . 

Is this issue is due to finish end date 25-08-2024 ?

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori - App Variant title translation
entry.content:
Hi @piotrkocakmd 

1. Yes, it is possible to have key user app variant titles translated into other languages! App variants use standard translation mechanisms. The specific tools depend on your backend system. Which system are you using, is it S/4HANA, S/4HANA Public Cloud, BTP ABAP environment or BTP Cloud Foundry?

2. We recommend changing your title to better distinguish between the original app and the app variant.

entry.title: Re: Data Binding with Repeating Items and Simple Tables in Print Forms
entry.content:
Hi Felix,
thanks a lot for this very informative blog post. I'm new to Adobe Live Cycle Designer and I'm stuck in the "Bind once Limitation". As a requirement I have four-five different tables but i need to use the same items (Bindings). For example the items that I used in Table3 will also be used in Table4 (screenshot). I am looking forward to next post with detailed descriptions. I'll also join the Live session today (22.08.2024), hope to meet there.

Thanks

entry.title: SAP DM - EWM Goods Receipt Failed
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have the following problem:

We have connected our ERP with SAP Digital Manufacturing and SAP Extended Warehouse Management. We would now like to post a goods receipt in the order POD after completing the SFC and material consumption. Unfortunately, we get the following error:

We have tried various settings in the “Manage Next Numbers” app, unfortunately without success. It seems to be an inconsistency with the HU/SSCC Settings. We have used the settings from the SAP Guide for Advanced Production Integration and MES-driven Staging. 

Please let me know if you have any ideas.

thank you and best regards

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation Deployment Time Out
entry.content:
Hi all,

Hope everyone is well.

I have created a project in SAP Build Process Automation. The release went fine but every time I try to Deploy it, it gives me the error "Deployment request timeout".

 

I though this could be due to the size of the project, but it happens the same for a dummy project without anything in it.

Is anyone having a similar issue? If so, did you find the cause of it?

 

Thanks,

Victor

entry.title: Re: xfp output of Adobe form to be converted pdf
entry.content:
Sandra, yes, as you said, pdf spool will generate by the Adobe form thru output type. But I want to display the Adobe form on the SAP Screen, for which, I am triggering the stand-alone program, without going for output type, in which, I have called Adobe form and the function modules, FP_JOB_OPEN, Adobe form Function Module and FP_JOB_CLOSE. 

         I am getting the output in XFP format, which I want to display as PDF, for which I am using the 'CONVERT_ABAPSPOOLJOB_2_PDF. But this FM is giving the error that, job type is not ADS one, when I checked the spool request job type, its coming as 'BINARY'.

        spool request data is coming as,

       Instead of the above one, I want to display as layout with data.

Rgds,

G. Khadeer Basha
